hero.title: Your plant has feelings.
hero.subtitle: Are you listening?
hero.description: Plantagoshi is a smart IoT planter with an AI soul — it senses your plant's health and expresses its mood in real time through an integrated screen and a companion app.
hero.badge: Winner — 28th Défi OSEntreprendre 2026, Student Category (MRC de Pierre-De Saurel)

quotes.happy: I'm living my best life right now.
quotes.content: All good here. Don't mind me growing.
quotes.thirsty: My roots are feeling a bit crunchy. Water?
quotes.angry: It is WAY too hot. Maybe move me?
quotes.sleepy: Shhh… dreaming of rain. Good night.

features.label: The Hardware
features.title: More than a Pot. A Companion.
features.description: Built from scratch with custom PCB design, embedded firmware, and a dedicated AI — this is a full hardware + software student project.

precision.title: Precision Sensing
precision.desc: Monitors soil moisture, ambient light (lux), temperature, and humidity 24/7 using calibrated sensors embedded in the custom PCB.

ai.title: AI Plant Personality
ai.desc: Raw sensor data is processed by our API to generate expressive, first-person plant responses — each plant has a unique voice.

app.title: Remote Care App
app.desc: Chat with your plant, trigger watering, view live data, and manage multiple plants from anywhere via our companion mobile app.

appShowcase.label: The Software
appShowcase.title: Monitor, Chat, and Care — All in One App
appShowcase.description: The Plantagoshi companion app gives you full visibility on your plant's environment and lets you interact with it through AI-powered conversations.
features: Real-time sensor data: temperature, luminosity, and water level
24-hour historical graphs with min, average, and max values
AI chat to communicate with your plant via text or audio
Multiple AI personalities to give each plant a unique voice
Multi-plant dashboard to manage all your connected pots

timeline.label: Our Journey
timeline.title: From Wires to Wonder
timeline.description: A student project built over several months of iteration and learning.

kickoff.note: Beginning of the Plantagoshi project. Initial research into IoT plant monitoring, sensor selection, and early software architecture planning.

prototype.note: First working prototype with soil moisture, light, and temperature sensors. 3D-printed enclosure and Raspberry Pi as the main controller.

pcb.note: First custom PCB electrical design. Moved from Raspberry Pi to a dedicated board for better power efficiency and a smaller form factor.

screen.note: Switched to an ESP32 with an integrated screen. The pot now displays its mood directly, with expressive faces reacting to sensor data in real time.

osentreprendre.note: Plantagoshi was selected as the winning project in the Student category at the 28th Défi OSEntreprendre, local level for the MRC de Pierre-De Saurel. Now advancing to the regional Montérégie level.

team.label: The Makers
team.title: Built by Students, for Plant Lovers
team.description: A passionate team of students from your school combining hardware, software, and a love of plants.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: Have a question about Plantagoshi? Want to collaborate or learn more? Drop us a message.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind...
contact.note: We'll get back to you as soon as we can.
contact.successMessage: Thanks for reaching out. We'll get back to you soon.
